<div class="page">

# WireGuard IN Ubuntu

\

WireGuard is a modern VPN protocol that uses cutting-edge cryptography
generation. It is designed to be:

- **Light and fast**
- **Easy to configure**
- **Secure** (use Curve25519, ChaCha20, Poly1305, etc.)
- **Integrated into the Linux kernel** since version 5.6

To install on Ubuntu:

<div class="codebox">

    sudo apt update
    sudo apt install wireguard

</div>

This will install:

- The kernel module (`wireguard`)
- Configuration tools (`wg`, `wg-quick`)

Once installed on both computers, it is time to generate the keys

## Keys Generation

### Simple option:

In the first node:

<div class="codebox">

    wg genkey | tee privatekeyA | wg pubkey > publickeyA

</div>

In the second node:

<div class="codebox">

    wg genkey | tee privatekeyB | wg pubkey > publickeyB

</div>

### More professional option:

At each node:

<div class="codebox">

    # Generar clave privada
    wg genkey | sudo tee /etc/wireguard/private.key
    # Generar clave pública
    sudo cat /etc/wireguard/private.key | wg pubkey | sudo tee /etc/wireguard/public.key
    # Proteger la clave privada
    sudo chmod 600 /etc/wireguard/private.key

</div>

## Into node A (Public IP 10.10.10.7 / Tunnel IP 192.168.200.1/30)

We need the VMs to be able to communicate via host-only **only for the
WireGuard traffic** (port 51820), but all other traffic
go through the tunnel. Therefore we must have to assign the IPs in mask
24 and 32, having to be /32, the first.

To do this, we eliminate /24 and put /32:

<div class="codebox">

    # Elimina la IP de la interfaz host-only 
    sudo ip addr del 10.10.10.7/24 dev enp0s8  
    # Añade solo una IP sin la ruta de red completa 
    sudo ip addr add 10.10.10.7/32 dev enp0s8  
    # Mantén la interfaz UP 
    sudo ip link set enp0s8 up
    # Restaurar IP con máscara de red 
    sudo ip addr add 10.10.10.7/24 dev enp0s8 2>/dev/null || true

</div>

The /etc/wireguard/wg2 file

<div class="codebox">

    [Interface] 
        # Clave privada del Nodo A
        PrivateKey = SHkPfgUDdpHIX97NgQ4PhHyMmOSw/SefhHIBoCaBCFo= 
        Address = 192.168.200.1/30 
        ListenPort = 51820 
        Table = 200

        # Ruta para el peer a través de WireGuard (No es necesaria porque la crea automáticamente)
        #PostUp = ip route add 10.10.10.155/32 dev wg2 table 200 
        # EXCEPCIÓN: permitir tráfico WireGuard por host-only
        PostUp = ip rule add to 10.10.10.155 iif wg2 lookup main priority 40 
        PostUp = ip rule add from 10.10.10.155 iif wg2 lookup main priority 40 
        # Reglas para forzar el resto del tráfico por WireGuard
        PostUp = ip rule add to 10.10.10.155/32 lookup 200 priority 50 
        PostUp = ip rule add from 10.10.10.155/32 lookup 200 priority 50

        #PostDown = ip route del 10.10.10.155/32 dev wg2 table 200 
        PostDown = ip rule del to 10.10.10.155 iif wg2 lookup main priority 40 
        PostDown = ip rule del from 10.10.10.155 iif wg2 lookup main priority 40 
        PostDown = ip rule del to 10.10.10.155/32 lookup 200 priority 50 
        PostDown = ip rule del from 10.10.10.155/32 lookup 200 priority 50 
        #PostDown = echo 0 > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/wg2/proxy_arp 

    [Peer] 
        # Clave pública del nodo B
        PublicKey = GD9vP5Nb0AssJZGyrYCIEd4NxuJmXSpHepHosDR0b3k= 
        Endpoint = 10.10.10.155:51820 
        AllowedIPs = 10.10.10.155/32, 192.168.200.2/32 
        PersistentKeepalive = 25

</div>

## Into Node B (Publlic IP 10.10.10.155 / Tunel IP 192.168.200.2/30)

To do this, we eliminate /24 and put /32:

<div class="codebox">

    # Elimina la IP de la interfaz host-only 
    sudo ip addr del 10.10.10.155/24 dev enp0s8  
    # Añade solo una IP sin la ruta de red completa 
    sudo ip addr add 10.10.10.155/32 dev enp0s8  
    # Mantén la interfaz UP 
    sudo ip link set enp0s8 up
    # Restaurar IP con máscara de red 
    sudo ip addr add 10.10.10.155/24 dev enp0s8 2>/dev/null || true

</div>

The /etc/wireguard/wg2 file

<div class="codebox">

    [Interface]
        # Clave privada del Nodo B
        PrivateKey = uD/Dttrkm2Ma8RHCRSruu3BJDGAdWGMFkRs1MUNL0kA=
        Address = 192.168.200.2/30
        ListenPort = 51820
        Table = 200

        # Ruta para el peer a través de WireGuard (No es necesaria porque la crea automáticamente)
        #PostUp = ip route add 10.10.10.7/32 dev wg2 table 200
        # EXCEPCIÓN: permitir tráfico WireGuard por host-only
        PostUp = ip rule add to 10.10.10.7 ipproto udp dport 51820 lookup main priority 40
        PostUp = ip rule add from 10.10.10.7 ipproto udp sport 51820 lookup main priority 40
        # Reglas para forzar el resto del tráfico por WireGuard
        PostUp = ip rule add to 10.10.10.7/32 lookup 200 priority 50
        PostUp = ip rule add from 10.10.10.7/32 lookup 200 priority 50

    PostDown = ip rule del to 10.10.10.7 ipproto udp dport 51820 lookup main priority 40
        PostDown = ip rule del from 10.10.10.7 ipproto udp sport 51820 lookup main priority 40
        PostDown = ip rule del to 10.10.10.7/32 lookup 200 priority 50
        PostDown = ip rule del from 10.10.10.7/32 lookup 200 priority 50

    [Peer]
        # Clave pública del nodo A
        PublicKey = rYxQxiJBzP8SfajkyVhLheo9Ixp7devsFC7JDjC69RQ=
        Endpoint = 10.10.10.7:51820
        AllowedIPs = 10.10.10.7/32, 192.168.200.1/32
        PersistentKeepalive = 25

</div>

We activate Wireguard on both nodes
with**<span style="color:#ff9d00;">:</span>**

<div class="codebox">

    sudo wg-quick up wg2

</div>

Optionally, we can open the firewall:

<div class="codebox">

    sudo ufw allow 51820/udp

</div>

### Comprobaciones en el nodo B:

<div class="codebox">

    $ sudo wg show
    interface: wg2
      public key: GD9vP5Nb0AssJZGyrYcIEd4NxuJmXSpFheposDROb3k=
      private key: (hidden)
      listening port: 51820

    peer: rYxQxiJBzP8SfajkyVhLheo9Ixp7devsFC7JDjC69RQ=
        endpoint: 10.10.10.7:51820
        allowed ips: 10.10.10.7/32, 192.168.200.1/32
        latest handshake: 2 minutes, 26 seconds ago
        transfer: 10.52 KiB received, 9.99 KiB sent
        persistent keepalive: every 25 seconds

</div>

<div class="codebox">

    $ ip rule show
    0: from all local lookup
    40: from all to 10.10.10.7 ipproto udp dport 51820 lookup main
    40: from 10.10.10.7 ipproto udp sport 51820 lookup main
    50: from all to 10.10.10.7 lookup 200
    50: from 10.10.10.7 lookup 200
    32766: from all lookup main
    32767: from all lookup default

</div>

<div class="codebox">

    $ sudo ip route show table 200
    10.10.10.7 dev wg2 scope link 
    192.168.200.1 dev wg2 scope link

</div>

<div class="codebox">

    $ sudo ip route
    default via 10.0.2.2 dev enp0s3 proto dhcp src 10.0.2.15 metric 100 
    default via 10.10.10.1 dev enp0s8 proto static metric 20101 
    10.0.2.0/24 dev enp0s3 proto kernel scope link src 10.0.2.15 metric 100 
    10.10.10.0/24 dev enp0s8 proto kernel scope link src 10.10.10.155 
    10.10.10.0/24 dev enp0s8 proto kernel scope link src 10.10.10.155 metric 101 
    10.33.33.0/24 dev br-ogs proto kernel scope link src 10.33.33.1 linkdown 
    172.17.0.0/16 dev docker0 proto kernel scope link src 172.17.0.1 linkdown 
    172.18.0.0/16 dev br-ae5c6cd9d2fd proto kernel scope link src 172.18.0.1 
    192.168.70.128/26 dev demo-oai proto kernel scope link src 192.168.70.129 
    192.168.122.0/24 dev virbr0 proto kernel scope link src 192.168.122.1 linkdown 
    192.168.200.0/30 dev wg2 proto kernel scope link src 192.168.200.2

</div>

If we capture both ENP0S8 and WG2 we can see that by the
host-only interface we only see traffic
[WireGuard](5GTACTIC--MITM--WireGuard_136.html) encrypted, while
In WG2 we already have the traffic between both ends clear.

[![](../images/138-1.png)](data:image/png;base64,)

</div>
